mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) directly captures consumer expenditure momentum; because consumption is the largest component of aggregate demand, this momentum translates into higher expected corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward test shows that top quintile of RSAFS monthly changes does not outperform bottom quintile over 20-day horizons (Rank IC < 0.02) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% anchors short-term discount rates at the effective lower bound, reducing the opportunity cost of equity and forcing investors down the risk curve; this compression in the equity risk premium produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal's hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in the unemployment rate and consumer prices generate a stagflationary impulse: rising joblessness erodes household income while higher input costs squeeze corporate margins. This joint deterioration in real activity and pricing power drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined long-short portfolio sorted on UNRATE and CPIAUCSL joint direction fails to produce negative 20-day returns in > 50% of co-rising instances over any 3-year rolling window, or interaction term t-stat < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, labor markets operate near capacity; a cumulative rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within three months signals that the economy has crossed an inflection point into contraction. This deterioration in the employment outlook triggers risk-off repositioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle regimes identified by PAYEMS growth deceleration, triggered UNRATE spikes fail to precede negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances, or the strategy Sharpe ratio is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which lifts near-term earnings expectations and operating leverage. The market prices this momentum quickly, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Top quintile month-over-month INDPRO changes do not generate statistically higher 5-day forward returns than the bottom quintile (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) serve as a high-beta leading indicator of residential investment and construction employment. When starts fall below 1,000 thousand units, the shortfall signals an impending contraction in credit-sensitive durable demand and downstream consumption, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with HOUST below 1,000 do not produce significantly negative 20-day S&P 500 returns compared to periods above 1,000 (two-sample t-test p > 0.05) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate concurrent with a month-over-month decline in industrial production represents monetary tightening into a slowing real economy. This policy-economy mismatch amplifies refinancing stress and earnings downgrade risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction portfolio (FEDFUNDS up, INDPRO down) fails to underperform the opposite joint regime by more than 3% annualized over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term in a panel regression has p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) is the most comprehensive monthly measure of labor income generation; above-trend month-over-month gains signal accelerating aggregate demand through the wage-income channel and reduce recession probability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Top decile PAYEMS monthly changes do not generate positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the strategy's information coefficient falls below 0.02 in any 2-year rolling window.
